You have an `upstream` remote pointing to the original `Open-ACP/OpenACP` repo. Here's the plan:

1. Fetch from upstream
2. Merge upstream changes into `main` and `develop`
3. Push to your origin fork

Want me to sync both `main` and `develop` branches? Also, do you prefer **merge** or **rebase** when incorporating upstream changes?

If you just want a straightforward merge sync, I can run:

```
git fetch upstream
git checkout main && git merge upstream/main
git checkout develop && git merge upstream/develop
git push origin main develop
```

Shall I proceed?

    22â†’pnpm --filter @openacp/adapter-telegram build
    23â†’```
    24â†’
    25â†’No test framework or linter is configured yet.
    26â†’
    27â†’## Monorepo Structure
    28â†’
    29â†’pnpm workspaces with two package locations:
    30â†’- `packages/core` â€” `@openacp/core`: orchestrator, session/agent lifecycle, config, types
    31â†’- `packages/adapters/*` â€” channel adapters (currently only `@openacp/adapter-telegram`)
    32â†’
    33â†’Adapters depend on core via `workspace:*`. The root `package.json` has a dependency on `@zed-industries/claude-agent-acp`.
    34â†’

    36â†’
    37â†’**Request flow:** User message â†’ ChannelAdapter â†’ `OpenACPCore.handleMessage()` â†’ `SessionManager` (lookup/create session) â†’ `Session.prompt()` â†’ `AgentInstance.prompt()` (ACP SDK) â†’ agent subprocess. Responses stream back as `AgentEvent`s, converted to `OutgoingMessage`s, and sent to the adapter.
    38â†’
    39â†’**Key classes in `packages/core/src/`:**
    40â†’- `OpenACPCore` (`core.ts`) â€” top-level orchestrator; registers adapters, wires session events
    41â†’- `AgentManager` (`agent-manager.ts`) â€” spawns ACP agent subprocesses
    42â†’- `AgentInstance` (`agent-instance.ts`) â€” wraps ACP `ClientSideConnection`; implements the Client interface (sessionUpdate, requestPermission, file/terminal ops)
    43â†’- `SessionManager` (`session-manager.ts`) â€” maps (channelId, threadId) â†’ Session
    44â†’- `Session` (`session.ts`) â€” conversation state with prompt queue and auto-naming
    45â†’- `ConfigManager` (`config.ts`) â€” loads `~/.openacp/config.json` with Zod validation; env var overrides (`OPENACP_TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN`, etc.)
    46â†’- `ChannelAdapter` (`channel.ts`) â€” abstract base class that adapters extend
    47â†’

    49â†’- `TelegramAdapter` (`adapter.ts`) â€” extends `ChannelAdapter`; uses grammy
    50â†’- `MessageDraft` (`streaming.ts`) â€” buffers streaming responses with 1-second throttle
    51â†’- `PermissionHandler` (`permissions.ts`) â€” inline keyboard buttons for agent permission requests
    52â†’- `Commands` (`commands.ts`) â€” bot commands: `/new`, `/new_chat`, `/cancel`, `/status`, `/agents`, `/help`
    53â†’
    54â†’**ACP integration:** Agents are subprocesses communicating via stdio ndjson. `AgentInstance` creates a `ClientSideConnection` from `@agentclientprotocol/sdk` and handles all ACP client callbacks.
    55â†’
    56â†’## TypeScript Configuration
    57â†’
    58â†’- Target: ES2022, Module: NodeNext, strict mode
    59â†’- All packages are ES modules (`"type": "module"`)
    60â†’- Base config in `tsconfig.base.json`; packages extend it
    61â†’
    62â†’## Configuration
    63â†’
    64â†’App config lives at `~/.openacp/config.json`. Schema defined with Zod in `config.ts`. Key sections: `channels.telegram`, `agents` (name â†’ command/args/workingDirectory/env), `defaultAgent`, `workspace.baseDir`, `security` (allowedUserIds, maxConcurrentSessions, sessionTimeoutMinutes).
    65â†’
    66â†’Environment variable overrides: `OPENACP_CONFIG_PATH`, `OPENACP_TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN`, `OPENACP_TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID`, `OPENACP_WORKSPACE_DIR`, `OPENACP_DEBUG`.
    67â†’
    68â†’## Core Types (`packages/core/src/types.ts`)
    69â†’
    70â†’- `IncomingMessage` â€” channelId, threadId, userId, text
    71â†’- `OutgoingMessage` â€” type (text, thought, tool_call, tool_update, plan, usage, session_end, error), text, metadata
    72â†’- `AgentEvent` â€” union of event types streamed from the agent
    73â†’- `PermissionRequest` / `NotificationMessage` â€” for permission UI and notifications
    74â†’=======
    75â†’## Build & Run
    76â†’
    77â†’```bash
    78â†’pnpm install            # Install dependencies
    79â†’pnpm build              # TypeScript compile (tsc)
    80â†’pnpm build:publish      # Bundle for npm publish (tsup â†’ dist-publish/)
    81â†’pnpm start              # Run: node dist/cli.js
    82â†’pnpm dev                # Watch mode (tsc --watch)
    83â†’pnpm test               # Run tests (vitest)
    84â†’```
    85â†’
    86â†’## Architecture
    87â†’
    88â†’OpenACP bridges AI coding agents to messaging platforms via the Agent Client Protocol (ACP). The flow:
    89â†’
    90â†’```
    91â†’User (Telegram) â†’ ChannelAdapter â†’ OpenACPCore â†’ Session â†’ AgentInstance (ACP subprocess)
    92â†’```
    93â†’
    94â†’### Project Layout
    95â†’
    96â†’```
    97â†’src/
    98â†’  cli.ts              â€” CLI entry (start, install, uninstall, plugins, --version, --help)
    99â†’  main.ts             â€” Server startup, adapter registration
   100â†’  index.ts            â€” Public API exports
   101â†’  core/               â€” Core modules
   102â†’    config.ts         â€” Zod-validated config from ~/.openacp/config.json
   103â†’    core.ts           â€” OpenACPCore orchestrator
   104â†’    session.ts        â€” Session with prompt queue, auto-naming
   105â†’    agent-instance.ts â€” ACP subprocess client
   106â†’    channel.ts        â€” ChannelAdapter abstract base
   107â†’    plugin-manager.ts â€” Plugin install/uninstall/load from ~/.openacp/plugins/
   108â†’    setup.ts          â€” Interactive first-run setup
   109â†’  adapters/
   110â†’    telegram/         â€” Built-in Telegram adapter (grammY)
   111â†’```
   112â†’
   113â†’### Core Abstractions
   114â†’
   115â†’**OpenACPCore** (`core.ts`) â€” Registers adapters, routes messages, creates sessions, wires agent events to adapters. Enforces security (allowedUserIds, maxConcurrentSessions).
   116â†’
   117â†’**Session** (`session.ts`) â€” Wraps an AgentInstance with a prompt queue (serial processing), auto-naming (asks agent to summarize after first prompt), and lifecycle management.
   118â†’
   119â†’**AgentInstance** (`agent-instance.ts`) â€” Spawns agent subprocess, implements full ACP Client interface. Converts ACP events to AgentEvent types.
   120â†’
   121â†’**ChannelAdapter** (`channel.ts`) â€” Abstract base. Implementations must handle: sendMessage, sendPermissionRequest, sendNotification, createSessionThread, renameSessionThread.
   122â†’
   123â†’**ConfigManager** (`config.ts`) â€” Zod-validated config. Supports env overrides: `OPENACP_TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN`, `OPENACP_TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID`, `OPENACP_DEFAULT_AGENT`, `OPENACP_DEBUG`.
   124â†’
   125â†’### Telegram Adapter Patterns
   126â†’
   127â†’- **Forum topics**: Each session gets its own topic. System topics: Notifications, Assistant
   128â†’- **MessageDraft** (`streaming.ts`): Buffers text chunks, sends periodic batch updates
   129â†’- **Callback routing**: Permission buttons use `p:` prefix, menu buttons use `m:` prefix. Must use `bot.callbackQuery(/^prefix/)` to avoid blocking the middleware chain
   130â†’- **Topic-first creation**: Create forum topic BEFORE `core.handleNewSession()` to prevent race condition
   131â†’
   132â†’## npm Publishing
   133â†’
   134â†’Published as `@openacp/cli` on npm. Users install with `npm install -g @openacp/cli`.
   135â†’
   136â†’- `pnpm build:publish` bundles via tsup into `dist-publish/`
   137â†’- GitHub Action auto-publishes on tag push (`v*`)
   138â†’- Plugin system: `openacp install @openacp/adapter-discord` installs to `~/.openacp/plugins/`
   139â†’
   140â†’## Conventions
   141â†’
   142â†’- ESM-only (`"type": "module"`), all imports use `.js` extension
   143â†’- TypeScript strict mode, target ES2022, NodeNext module resolution
   144â†’>>>>>>> upstream/main

file.content:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

<<<<<<< HEAD
## What is OpenACP

A self-hosted bridge that connects AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex) to messaging platforms (Telegram, Discord) via the Agent Client Protocol (ACP). Users send messages in Telegram; OpenACP routes them to AI agent subprocesses over ACP's JSON-RPC/stdio transport.

## Build & Run Commands

```bash
pnpm install          # Install all workspace dependencies
pnpm build            # Build all packages (runs tsc in each)
pnpm dev              # Watch-mode build for core package
pnpm start            # Run the server (node packages/core/dist/main.js)
```

Build a single package:
```bash
pnpm --filter @openacp/core build
pnpm --filter @openacp/adapter-telegram build
```

No test framework or linter is configured yet.

## Monorepo Structure

pnpm workspaces with two package locations:
- `packages/core` â€” `@openacp/core`: orchestrator, session/agent lifecycle, config, types
- `packages/adapters/*` â€” channel adapters (currently only `@openacp/adapter-telegram`)

Adapters depend on core via `workspace:*`. The root `package.json` has a dependency on `@zed-industries/claude-agent-acp`.

## Architecture

**Request flow:** User message â†’ ChannelAdapter â†’ `OpenACPCore.handleMessage()` â†’ `SessionManager` (lookup/create session) â†’ `Session.prompt()` â†’ `AgentInstance.prompt()` (ACP SDK) â†’ agent subprocess. Responses stream back as `AgentEvent`s, converted to `OutgoingMessage`s, and sent to the adapter.

**Key classes in `packages/core/src/`:**
- `OpenACPCore` (`core.ts`) â€” top-level orchestrator; registers adapters, wires session events
- `AgentManager` (`agent-manager.ts`) â€” spawns ACP agent subprocesses
- `AgentInstance` (`agent-instance.ts`) â€” wraps ACP `ClientSideConnection`; implements the Client interface (sessionUpdate, requestPermission, file/terminal ops)
- `SessionManager` (`session-manager.ts`) â€” maps (channelId, threadId) â†’ Session
- `Session` (`session.ts`) â€” conversation state with prompt queue and auto-naming
- `ConfigManager` (`config.ts`) â€” loads `~/.openacp/config.json` with Zod validation; env var overrides (`OPENACP_TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN`, etc.)
- `ChannelAdapter` (`channel.ts`) â€” abstract base class that adapters extend

**Telegram adapter (`packages/adapters/telegram/src/`):**
- `TelegramAdapter` (`adapter.ts`) â€” extends `ChannelAdapter`; uses grammy
- `MessageDraft` (`streaming.ts`) â€” buffers streaming responses with 1-second throttle
- `PermissionHandler` (`permissions.ts`) â€” inline keyboard buttons for agent permission requests
- `Commands` (`commands.ts`) â€” bot commands: `/new`, `/new_chat`, `/cancel`, `/status`, `/agents`, `/help`

**ACP integration:** Agents are subprocesses communicating via stdio ndjson. `AgentInstance` creates a `ClientSideConnection` from `@agentclientprotocol/sdk` and handles all ACP client callbacks.

## TypeScript Configuration

- Target: ES2022, Module: NodeNext, strict mode
- All packages are ES modules (`"type": "module"`)
- Base config in `tsconfig.base.json`; packages extend it

## Configuration

App config lives at `~/.openacp/config.json`. Schema defined with Zod in `config.ts`. Key sections: `channels.telegram`, `agents` (name â†’ command/args/workingDirectory/env), `defaultAgent`, `workspace.baseDir`, `security` (allowedUserIds, maxConcurrentSessions, sessionTimeoutMinutes).

Environment variable overrides: `OPENACP_CONFIG_PATH`, `OPENACP_TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN`, `OPENACP_TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID`, `OPENACP_WORKSPACE_DIR`, `OPENACP_DEBUG`.

## Core Types (`packages/core/src/types.ts`)

- `IncomingMessage` â€” channelId, threadId, userId, text
- `OutgoingMessage` â€” type (text, thought, tool_call, tool_update, plan, usage, session_end, error), text, metadata
- `AgentEvent` â€” union of event types streamed from the agent
- `PermissionRequest` / `NotificationMessage` â€” for permission UI and notifications
=======
## Build & Run

```bash
pnpm install            # Install dependencies
pnpm build              # TypeScript compile (tsc)
pnpm build:publish      # Bundle for npm publish (tsup â†’ dist-publish/)
pnpm start              # Run: node dist/cli.js
pnpm dev                # Watch mode (tsc --watch)
pnpm test               # Run tests (vitest)
```

## Architecture

OpenACP bridges AI coding agents to messaging platforms via the Agent Client Protocol (ACP). The flow:

```
User (Telegram) â†’ ChannelAdapter â†’ OpenACPCore â†’ Session â†’ AgentInstance (ACP subprocess)
```

### Project Layout

```
src/
  cli.ts              â€” CLI entry (start, install, uninstall, plugins, --version, --help)
  main.ts             â€” Server startup, adapter registration
  index.ts            â€” Public API exports
  core/               â€” Core modules
    config.ts         â€” Zod-validated config from ~/.openacp/config.json
    core.ts           â€” OpenACPCore orchestrator
    session.ts        â€” Session with prompt queue, auto-naming
    agent-instance.ts â€” ACP subprocess client
    channel.ts        â€” ChannelAdapter abstract base
    plugin-manager.ts â€” Plugin install/uninstall/load from ~/.openacp/plugins/
    setup.ts          â€” Interactive first-run setup
  adapters/
    telegram/         â€” Built-in Telegram adapter (grammY)
```

### Core Abstractions

**OpenACPCore** (`core.ts`) â€” Registers adapters, routes messages, creates sessions, wires agent events to adapters. Enforces security (allowedUserIds, maxConcurrentSessions).

**Session** (`session.ts`) â€” Wraps an AgentInstance with a prompt queue (serial processing), auto-naming (asks agent to summarize after first prompt), and lifecycle management.

**AgentInstance** (`agent-instance.ts`) â€” Spawns agent subprocess, implements full ACP Client interface. Converts ACP events to AgentEvent types.

**ChannelAdapter** (`channel.ts`) â€” Abstract base. Implementations must handle: sendMessage, sendPermissionRequest, sendNotification, createSessionThread, renameSessionThread.

**ConfigManager** (`config.ts`) â€” Zod-validated config. Supports env overrides: `OPENACP_TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN`, `OPENACP_TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID`, `OPENACP_DEFAULT_AGENT`, `OPENACP_DEBUG`.

### Telegram Adapter Patterns

- **Forum topics**: Each session gets its own topic. System topics: Notifications, Assistant
- **MessageDraft** (`streaming.ts`): Buffers text chunks, sends periodic batch updates
- **Callback routing**: Permission buttons use `p:` prefix, menu buttons use `m:` prefix. Must use `bot.callbackQuery(/^prefix/)` to avoid blocking the middleware chain
- **Topic-first creation**: Create forum topic BEFORE `core.handleNewSession()` to prevent race condition

## npm Publishing

Published as `@openacp/cli` on npm. Users install with `npm install -g @openacp/cli`.

- `pnpm build:publish` bundles via tsup into `dist-publish/`
- GitHub Action auto-publishes on tag push (`v*`)
- Plugin system: `openacp install @openacp/adapter-discord` installs to `~/.openacp/plugins/`

## Conventions

- ESM-only (`"type": "module"`), all imports use `.js` extension
- TypeScript strict mode, target ES2022, NodeNext module resolution
>>>>>>> upstream/main

content.text: I'll keep the upstream version (lines 75-143) since it matches the current repo structure.

input.content:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Build & Run

```bash
pnpm install            # Install dependencies
pnpm build              # TypeScript compile (tsc)
pnpm build:publish      # Bundle for npm publish (tsup â†’ dist-publish/)
pnpm start              # Run: node dist/cli.js
pnpm dev                # Watch mode (tsc --watch)
pnpm test               # Run tests (vitest)
```

## Architecture

OpenACP bridges AI coding agents to messaging platforms via the Agent Client Protocol (ACP). The flow:

```
User (Telegram) â†’ ChannelAdapter â†’ OpenACPCore â†’ Session â†’ AgentInstance (ACP subprocess)
```

### Project Layout

```
src/
  cli.ts              â€” CLI entry (start, install, uninstall, plugins, --version, --help)
  main.ts             â€” Server startup, adapter registration
  index.ts            â€” Public API exports
  core/               â€” Core modules
    config.ts         â€” Zod-validated config from ~/.openacp/config.json
    core.ts           â€” OpenACPCore orchestrator
    session.ts        â€” Session with prompt queue, auto-naming
    agent-instance.ts â€” ACP subprocess client
    channel.ts        â€” ChannelAdapter abstract base
    plugin-manager.ts â€” Plugin install/uninstall/load from ~/.openacp/plugins/
    setup.ts          â€” Interactive first-run setup
  adapters/
    telegram/         â€” Built-in Telegram adapter (grammY)
```

### Core Abstractions

**OpenACPCore** (`core.ts`) â€” Registers adapters, routes messages, creates sessions, wires agent events to adapters. Enforces security (allowedUserIds, maxConcurrentSessions).

**Session** (`session.ts`) â€” Wraps an AgentInstance with a prompt queue (serial processing), auto-naming (asks agent to summarize after first prompt), and lifecycle management.

**AgentInstance** (`agent-instance.ts`) â€” Spawns agent subprocess, implements full ACP Client interface. Converts ACP events to AgentEvent types.

**ChannelAdapter** (`channel.ts`) â€” Abstract base. Implementations must handle: sendMessage, sendPermissionRequest, sendNotification, createSessionThread, renameSessionThread.

**ConfigManager** (`config.ts`) â€” Zod-validated config. Supports env overrides: `OPENACP_TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN`, `OPENACP_TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID`, `OPENACP_DEFAULT_AGENT`, `OPENACP_DEBUG`.

### Telegram Adapter Patterns

- **Forum topics**: Each session gets its own topic. System topics: Notifications, Assistant
- **MessageDraft** (`streaming.ts`): Buffers text chunks, sends periodic batch updates
- **Callback routing**: Permission buttons use `p:` prefix, menu buttons use `m:` prefix. Must use `bot.callbackQuery(/^prefix/)` to avoid blocking the middleware chain
- **Topic-first creation**: Create forum topic BEFORE `core.handleNewSession()` to prevent race condition

## npm Publishing

Published as `@openacp/cli` on npm. Users install with `npm install -g @openacp/cli`.

- `pnpm build:publish` bundles via tsup into `dist-publish/`
- GitHub Action auto-publishes on tag push (`v*`)
- Plugin system: `openacp install @openacp/adapter-discord` installs to `~/.openacp/plugins/`

## Conventions

- ESM-only (`"type": "module"`), all imports use `.js` extension
- TypeScript strict mode, target ES2022, NodeNext module resolution

toolUseResult.content:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Build & Run

```bash
pnpm install            # Install dependencies
pnpm build              # TypeScript compile (tsc)
pnpm build:publish      # Bundle for npm publish (tsup â†’ dist-publish/)
pnpm start              # Run: node dist/cli.js
pnpm dev                # Watch mode (tsc --watch)
pnpm test               # Run tests (vitest)
```

## Architecture

OpenACP bridges AI coding agents to messaging platforms via the Agent Client Protocol (ACP). The flow:

```
User (Telegram) â†’ ChannelAdapter â†’ OpenACPCore â†’ Session â†’ AgentInstance (ACP subprocess)
```

### Project Layout

```
src/
  cli.ts              â€” CLI entry (start, install, uninstall, plugins, --version, --help)
  main.ts             â€” Server startup, adapter registration
  index.ts            â€” Public API exports
  core/               â€” Core modules
    config.ts         â€” Zod-validated config from ~/.openacp/config.json
    core.ts           â€” OpenACPCore orchestrator
    session.ts        â€” Session with prompt queue, auto-naming
    agent-instance.ts â€” ACP subprocess client
    channel.ts        â€” ChannelAdapter abstract base
    plugin-manager.ts â€” Plugin install/uninstall/load from ~/.openacp/plugins/
    setup.ts          â€” Interactive first-run setup
  adapters/
    telegram/         â€” Built-in Telegram adapter (grammY)
```

### Core Abstractions

**OpenACPCore** (`core.ts`) â€” Registers adapters, routes messages, creates sessions, wires agent events to adapters. Enforces security (allowedUserIds, maxConcurrentSessions).

**Session** (`session.ts`) â€” Wraps an AgentInstance with a prompt queue (serial processing), auto-naming (asks agent to summarize after first prompt), and lifecycle management.

**AgentInstance** (`agent-instance.ts`) â€” Spawns agent subprocess, implements full ACP Client interface. Converts ACP events to AgentEvent types.

**ChannelAdapter** (`channel.ts`) â€” Abstract base. Implementations must handle: sendMessage, sendPermissionRequest, sendNotification, createSessionThread, renameSessionThread.

**ConfigManager** (`config.ts`) â€” Zod-validated config. Supports env overrides: `OPENACP_TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN`, `OPENACP_TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID`, `OPENACP_DEFAULT_AGENT`, `OPENACP_DEBUG`.

### Telegram Adapter Patterns

- **Forum topics**: Each session gets its own topic. System topics: Notifications, Assistant
- **MessageDraft** (`streaming.ts`): Buffers text chunks, sends periodic batch updates
- **Callback routing**: Permission buttons use `p:` prefix, menu buttons use `m:` prefix. Must use `bot.callbackQuery(/^prefix/)` to avoid blocking the middleware chain
- **Topic-first creation**: Create forum topic BEFORE `core.handleNewSession()` to prevent race condition

## npm Publishing

Published as `@openacp/cli` on npm. Users install with `npm install -g @openacp/cli`.

- `pnpm build:publish` bundles via tsup into `dist-publish/`
- GitHub Action auto-publishes on tag push (`v*`)
- Plugin system: `openacp install @openacp/adapter-discord` installs to `~/.openacp/plugins/`

## Conventions

- ESM-only (`"type": "module"`), all imports use `.js` extension
- TypeScript strict mode, target ES2022, NodeNext module resolution

Responses stream back as `AgentEvent`s, converted to `OutgoingMessage`s, and sent to the adapter.
-- `OpenACPCore` (`core.ts`) â€” top-level orchestrator; registers adapters, wires session events
-- `AgentInstance` (`agent-instance.ts`) â€” wraps ACP `ClientSideConnection`; implements the Client interface (sessionUpdate, requestPermission, file/terminal ops)
-- `Session` (`session.ts`) â€” conversation state with prompt queue and auto-naming

-- `ChannelAdapter` (`channel.ts`) â€” abstract base class that adapters extend

-- Target: ES2022, Module: NodeNext, strict mode
-- Base config in `tsconfig.base.json`; packages extend it

-App config lives at `~/.openacp/config.json`. Schema defined with Zod in `config.ts`. Key sections: `channels.telegram`, `agents` (name â†’ command/args/workingDirectory/env), `defaultAgent`, `workspace.baseDir`, `security` (allowedUserIds, maxConcurrentSessions, sessionTimeoutMinutes).
-- `OutgoingMessage` â€” type (text, thought, tool_call, tool_update, plan, usage, session_end, error), text, metadata
-- `AgentEvent` â€” union of event types streamed from the agent
-- `PermissionRequest` / `NotificationMessage` â€” for permission UI and notifications
- TypeScript strict mode, target ES2022, NodeNext module resolution

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

<<<<<<< HEAD
## What is OpenACP

A self-hosted bridge that connects AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex) to messaging platforms (Telegram, Discord) via the Agent Client Protocol (ACP). Users send messages in Telegram; OpenACP routes them to AI agent subprocesses over ACP's JSON-RPC/stdio transport.

## Build & Run Commands

```bash
pnpm install          # Install all workspace dependencies
pnpm build            # Build all packages (runs tsc in each)
pnpm dev              # Watch-mode build for core package
pnpm start            # Run the server (node packages/core/dist/main.js)
```

Build a single package:
```bash
pnpm --filter @openacp/core build
pnpm --filter @openacp/adapter-telegram build
```

No test framework or linter is configured yet.

## Monorepo Structure

pnpm workspaces with two package locations:
- `packages/core` â€” `@openacp/core`: orchestrator, session/agent lifecycle, config, types
- `packages/adapters/*` â€” channel adapters (currently only `@openacp/adapter-telegram`)

Adapters depend on core via `workspace:*`. The root `package.json` has a dependency on `@zed-industries/claude-agent-acp`.

## Architecture

**Request flow:** User message â†’ ChannelAdapter â†’ `OpenACPCore.handleMessage()` â†’ `SessionManager` (lookup/create session) â†’ `Session.prompt()` â†’ `AgentInstance.prompt()` (ACP SDK) â†’ agent subprocess. Responses stream back as `AgentEvent`s, converted to `OutgoingMessage`s, and sent to the adapter.

**Key classes in `packages/core/src/`:**
- `OpenACPCore` (`core.ts`) â€” top-level orchestrator; registers adapters, wires session events
- `AgentManager` (`agent-manager.ts`) â€” spawns ACP agent subprocesses
- `AgentInstance` (`agent-instance.ts`) â€” wraps ACP `ClientSideConnection`; implements the Client interface (sessionUpdate, requestPermission, file/terminal ops)
- `SessionManager` (`session-manager.ts`) â€” maps (channelId, threadId) â†’ Session
- `Session` (`session.ts`) â€” conversation state with prompt queue and auto-naming
- `ConfigManager` (`config.ts`) â€” loads `~/.openacp/config.json` with Zod validation; env var overrides (`OPENACP_TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN`, etc.)
- `ChannelAdapter` (`channel.ts`) â€” abstract base class that adapters extend

**Telegram adapter (`packages/adapters/telegram/src/`):**
- `TelegramAdapter` (`adapter.ts`) â€” extends `ChannelAdapter`; uses grammy
- `MessageDraft` (`streaming.ts`) â€” buffers streaming responses with 1-second throttle
- `PermissionHandler` (`permissions.ts`) â€” inline keyboard buttons for agent permission requests
- `Commands` (`commands.ts`) â€” bot commands: `/new`, `/new_chat`, `/cancel`, `/status`, `/agents`, `/help`

**ACP integration:** Agents are subprocesses communicating via stdio ndjson. `AgentInstance` creates a `ClientSideConnection` from `@agentclientprotocol/sdk` and handles all ACP client callbacks.

## TypeScript Configuration

- Target: ES2022, Module: NodeNext, strict mode
- All packages are ES modules (`"type": "module"`)
- Base config in `tsconfig.base.json`; packages extend it

## Configuration

App config lives at `~/.openacp/config.json`. Schema defined with Zod in `config.ts`. Key sections: `channels.telegram`, `agents` (name â†’ command/args/workingDirectory/env), `defaultAgent`, `workspace.baseDir`, `security` (allowedUserIds, maxConcurrentSessions, sessionTimeoutMinutes).

Environment variable overrides: `OPENACP_CONFIG_PATH`, `OPENACP_TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN`, `OPENACP_TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID`, `OPENACP_WORKSPACE_DIR`, `OPENACP_DEBUG`.

## Core Types (`packages/core/src/types.ts`)

- `IncomingMessage` â€” channelId, threadId, userId, text
- `OutgoingMessage` â€” type (text, thought, tool_call, tool_update, plan, usage, session_end, error), text, metadata
- `AgentEvent` â€” union of event types streamed from the agent
- `PermissionRequest` / `NotificationMessage` â€” for permission UI and notifications
=======

```bash
pnpm build:publish      # Bundle for npm publish (tsup â†’ dist-publish/)
pnpm start              # Run: node dist/cli.js
pnpm dev                # Watch mode (tsc --watch)
pnpm test               # Run tests (vitest)
```

OpenACP bridges AI coding agents to messaging platforms via the Agent Client Protocol (ACP). The flow:

```

  adapters/
    telegram/         â€” Built-in Telegram adapter (grammY)
```

### Telegram Adapter Patterns

- **MessageDraft** (`streaming.ts`): Buffers text chunks, sends periodic batch updates

- **Topic-first creation**: Create forum topic BEFORE `core.handleNewSession()` to prevent race condition

## npm Publishing

- `pnpm build:publish` bundles via tsup into `dist-publish/`
- GitHub Action auto-publishes on tag push (`v*`)
- Plugin system: `openacp install @openacp/adapter-discord` installs to `~/.openacp/plugins/`

## Conventions

- ESM-only (`"type": "module"`), all imports use `.js` extension
- TypeScript strict mode, target ES2022, NodeNext module resolution
